consisting of beef cattle, horses and
mules, and the occasional ranches
wherever water is found testify as
to the fertility of the soil. 1t is
well known to ranchers and grang-
ers in the far west that wherever
you find sage brush you find rich
agricultural land. Sage brush never
grows in an alkali country and the
mere fact that sage grows is evil
dence that the soil is rich and if
water be applied it will become pro-
ductive. Sage of itself is not much
good except for grazing sheep and
the millions of head in Wyoming
live principally on the sage brush
prairies of the Great Rocky Noun
tain plateau. 1t is interesting to
note that large part of the coun-
try In Nevada to be opened by the
Western Pacific either Immediately
or later has recently been placed
within reach of water by canals and
iateralS from the Iruckee-CarSon
irrigation project upon which the
government is spending $4,000, 900
under the national irrigation act.
Work upon this project Is now
about completed and as the govern
ment fixes the farm unit at 500
acres for the Truckee-Carson pro-
ject the land which will be render
ed agriculturally available through
the development of this vast irriga-
ton system will not be gobbled up
by land syndicates, but will be cult
waited by bona fde resident home
steaders according to the spirit of
the new land act.
